title: Enginuity: Building an Open Multi-Domain Dataset of Complex Engineering Diagrams
abstract: Proposes Enginuity, the first open large-scale multi-domain engineering diagram dataset with comprehensive structural annotations for automated diagram parsing. Covers P&IDs, electrical schematics, and architectural drawings with >10K real-world diagrams containing both component and structural relationship annotations.
method_summary: Multi-domain annotation framework covering symbols, connections, and structural relationships across engineering diagram types. Provides standardized evaluation protocols for diagram understanding tasks.

title: GraphRAG for Engineering Diagrams
abstract: Combines LLMs with Retrieval-Augmented Generation and knowledge graphs for interacting with engineering diagrams such as P&IDs. Enables natural language querying of diagram content through graph-structured retrieval.
method_summary: Constructs knowledge graphs from digitized engineering diagrams, then uses GraphRAG to enable LLM-based natural language interaction with diagram content for maintenance and operational queries.

title: A Closed-Loop, Physics-Aware Agentic Framework for Auto-Generating Chemical Process and Instrumentation Diagrams
abstract: Physics-aware agentic framework for automatically generating PFDs and P&IDs while adhering to engineering constraints. Closed-loop design with verification against physical laws and engineering standards.
method_summary: Multi-agent system with physics-aware constraints for iterative P&ID generation, incorporating thermodynamic and process engineering validation in the generation loop.

title: Accelerating Manufacturing Scale-Up from Material Discovery Using Agentic Web Navigation and Retrieval-Augmented AI for Process Engineering Schematics Design
abstract: Uses agentic web navigation and RAG for process engineering schematics design, accelerating manufacturing scale-up from material discovery to PFD/PID generation.
method_summary: Agentic AI system combining web navigation for knowledge retrieval with RAG-based generation of process engineering schematics, bridging material discovery and manufacturing design.

title: Automatic Building Code Review
abstract: Framework for automated extraction of geometric attributes (surface area, tilt, insulation values), parsing of operational schedules, and compliance verification against building energy codes.
method_summary: Automated pipeline extracting geometric and operational attributes from building models and verifying compliance against regulatory codes using rule-based and ML-assisted checking.

title: Unified Vector Floorplan Generation via Markup Representation
abstract: Proposes a unified approach to residential floorplan generation using markup representation, bridging architecture and computer graphics for more efficient spatial design.
method_summary: Markup-based representation unifying different floorplan elements into a single generative framework, enabling end-to-end vector floorplan synthesis from constraints.

title: A Two-Level Codebook Based Network for End-to-End Vector Floorplan Generation
abstract: Two-level codebook network for automated floorplan generation, jointly modeling global spatial organization and precise geometric detail for end-to-end vector output.
method_summary: Hierarchical VQ-VAE with two-level codebooks: one for global room layout topology and one for fine-grained geometric details, enabling end-to-end vector floorplan generation.

title: Leveraging Vision-Language Models for Manufacturing Feature Recognition in CAD Designs
abstract: Investigates VLMs for automating recognition of manufacturing features in CAD designs without extensive training data. Evaluates GPT-4V and other VLMs on diverse manufacturing feature types, demonstrating zero-shot and few-shot capabilities.
method_summary: Evaluates pre-trained VLMs (GPT-4V, Claude) on manufacturing feature recognition from CAD renderings, comparing zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned approaches against traditional geometric rule-based methods.

title: Pointer-CAD: Unifying B-Rep and Command Sequences via Pointer-based Edges & Faces Selection
abstract: Unifies B-Rep and command sequence representations for CAD generation via pointer-based entity selection. Enables complex editing operations (fillet, chamfer) that require explicit face/edge selection, which prior command-sequence methods cannot support.
method_summary: Introduces pointer mechanism allowing LLM-based CAD generators to reference specific B-Rep entities (faces, edges) during command sequence generation, bridging the gap between parametric modeling and B-Rep representation.

title: HoLa: B-Rep Generation using a Holistic Latent Representation
abstract: Learns a holistic latent (HoLa) space for B-rep generation, enabling the first diffusion-based B-rep generator supporting diverse conditional inputs (text, point cloud, multi-view images, 2D sketches). Achieves greatly improved validity over state of the art.
method_summary: Reformulates B-rep topology as geometry of intersecting curves, creating a unified holistic latent space. Diffusion-based generator operates in this space for conditional and unconditional B-rep generation.

title: BrepVGAE: Variational Graph Autoencoder with Unified Latent Representation for B-rep
abstract: First variational graph autoencoder framework capable of holistically encoding and decoding B-rep models. Establishes unified formulation for heterogeneous faces and edges, overcoming limitations of conventional graph-based B-rep representations.
method_summary: Variational graph autoencoder with unified latent space for B-rep faces and edges, enabling both reconstruction and generation of valid B-rep models through graph-based encoding/decoding.

title: Few-Shot B-rep Classification with Hybrid Graph Representation
abstract: Addresses few-shot B-rep classification by constructing hybrid graph representations capturing multi-level structural information: local topology graph, global parallel graph, and regional association graph.
method_summary: Three-level hybrid graph representation (local topology, global parallel, regional association) combined with meta-learning for few-shot B-rep model classification with limited labeled data.

title: High-Fidelity B-Rep Latent Representation and Robust Generation
abstract: Builds compact high-fidelity latent representation for B-rep and reformulates validity constraints as differentiable objectives for robust generation. Achieves high-validity B-rep synthesis.
method_summary: Compact latent B-rep representation with differentiable validity constraints enabling robust generation of valid, high-fidelity B-rep models through constrained optimization.

title: BrepARG: AutoRegressive Generation with B-rep Holistic Token Sequence Representation
abstract: First attempt to encode B-rep geometry and topology into a holistic token sequence representation, enabling sequence-based B-rep generation with an autoregressive Transformer. Encodes B-rep into 3 types of tokens: geometry, position, and face index tokens.
method_summary: Holistic tokenization of B-rep into geometry tokens (face/edge UV-grids), position tokens (spatial coordinates), and face index tokens (topology). Autoregressive Transformer generates the unified sequence.

title: BrepGPT: Autoregressive B-rep Generation with Voronoi Half-Patch
abstract: Single-stage autoregressive framework for B-rep generation using Voronoi Half-Patch (VHP) representation. Decomposes B-reps into unified local units by assigning geometry to nearest half-edges and sampling their next pointers. Achieves state-of-the-art unconditional B-rep generation.
method_summary: Voronoi Half-Patch representation decomposes B-reps into unified local units. Single-stage autoregressive generation without cascaded multi-stage networks, reducing error accumulation.

title: AutoBrep: Autoregressive B-Rep Generation with Unified Topology and Geometry
abstract: Transformer model that autoregressively generates B-Reps with high quality and validity. Unified tokenization scheme encoding both geometric and topological characteristics as discrete tokens. Highly scalable to complex solids.
method_summary: Unified tokenization of B-rep geometry (surfaces, curves as Bezier patches) and topology (face-edge adjacency) into a single discrete token sequence. Decoder-only Transformer for autoregressive generation.

title: CADGCL: Unsupervised Retrieval of CAD Models via Boundary Representations
abstract: Unsupervised method for retrieving CAD models based on boundary representations. Transforms B-rep models into graph representations and applies graph contrastive learning for retrieval without labeled data.
method_summary: Converts B-rep to graphs, applies graph contrastive learning with augmentation strategies for unsupervised CAD model retrieval. No labeled training data required.

title: CAD-GPT: Synthesising CAD Construction Sequence with Spatial Reasoning-Enhanced Multimodal LLMs
abstract: Introduces CAD-GPT, a CAD synthesis method with spatial reasoning-enhanced MLLM that takes single image or text as input. Uses 3D Modeling Assistant Point (3D-MAP) for precise spatial inference in 3D modeling tasks.
method_summary: 3D Modeling Assistant Point (3D-MAP) representation enables precise spatial reasoning in MLLMs for CAD sequence generation. First MLLM specifically trained for CAD construction sequence synthesis.

title: GenCAD: Image-Conditioned Computer-Aided Design Generation with Transformer-Based Contrastive Representation and Diffusion Priors
abstract: Generative model using autoregressive transformers with contrastive learning and latent diffusion to transform image inputs into parametric CAD command sequences, producing editable 3D CAD models.
method_summary: Contrastive learning aligns image and CAD sequence embeddings, then latent diffusion generates CAD tokens conditioned on image features. Autoregressive transformer decodes the final CAD command sequence.

title: VideoCAD: A Large-Scale Video Dataset for Learning UI Interactions and 3D Reasoning from CAD Software
abstract: Large-scale synthetic dataset of over 41K annotated video recordings of CAD operations for learning long-horizon 3D CAD UI interactions from video. Automated framework for collecting high-fidelity UI action data from CAD software.
method_summary: Automated pipeline generating annotated CAD operation videos with keyframe extraction and action labeling. Enables training models for video-based CAD UI understanding and VQA tasks.

title: MiCADangelo: Fine-Grained Reconstruction of Constrained CAD Models from 3D Scans
abstract: Novel CAD reverse engineering method mimicking human design workflows using multi-plane 2D cross-sections to reconstruct detailed, editable, and fully parametric CAD models from 3D scans. First method incorporating sketch constraints directly into reconstruction.
method_summary: Analyzes 3D scans via 2D cross-sections to detect sketch planes, predicts constrained parametric sketches, and reconstructs fine-grained editable CAD models with geometric constraints.

title: CADCrafter: Generating Computer-Aided Design Models from Unconstrained Images
abstract: Image-to-parametric CAD model generation framework trained on synthetic data with geometry encoder and direct preference optimization to handle real-world unconstrained images. Bridges gap between synthetic training and real-world deployment.
method_summary: Geometry encoder extracts 3D-aware features from unconstrained images, combined with DPO alignment for robust CAD sequence generation. Trained on synthetic data, generalizes to real-world images.

title: CADDreamer: CAD Object Generation from Single-view Images
abstract: Generates CAD objects from single-view images using a primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model that perceives both local geometry and high-level structure for parametric CAD output.
method_summary: Primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model generates consistent multi-view representations from single images, then reconstructs parametric CAD models preserving geometric primitives.

title: Joint B-Rep Generation via Compositional k-Cell Particles
abstract: Encodes each topological entity as a composition of particles where adjacent cells share identical latents at interfaces, promoting geometric coupling along shared boundaries. Decouples rigid hierarchy for unified vertex/edge/face generation.
method_summary: Compositional k-cell particle representation where topology emerges from shared latent variables at cell interfaces. Enables joint generation of topology and geometry with global context awareness.
